 # Phase 01: Running Agents Orchestration Prototype
 
 This phase delivers the first end-to-end orchestration prototype for GatomIA by turning the existing running-agent, agent-chat, and cloud-agent surfaces into a single coherent experience that visibly tracks active sessions. It matters because it gives Product Engineers and Software Engineers a working orchestration dashboard immediately, proves the new direction with minimal refactoring risk, and creates the foundation that later phases will extend with hooks, visual flows, and autonomous task execution.
 
 - NEVER push de code
 
 ## Tasks
 
 - [x] Establish the orchestration prototype baseline by reusing existing extension and webview surfaces before creating new ones:
   - Inspect and map the current behavior in `src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts`, `src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.ts`, `src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts`, `src/panels/agent-chat-panel.ts`, `src/panels/cloud-agent-progress-panel.ts`, and `ui/src/components/cloud-agents/cloud-agent-progress-view.tsx`
   - Identify the smallest viable prototype that can be built by composing existing session state, registry events, and view registration instead of introducing a second orchestration stack
   - Capture the target slice in `docs/architecture/orchestration/running-agents-prototype.md` using Obsidian-style markdown with YAML front matter, links to `[[Running-Agents-Model]]` and `[[Orchestration-UI-Contract]]`, and explicit notes about reused modules versus new modules
   - Note: baseline mapping and reuse-first target slice captured on 2026-04-30 in `docs/architecture/orchestration/running-agents-prototype.md`
 
 - [x] Implement a unified orchestration read model for active and recent agents:
   - Reuse existing session and provider contracts before creating new types; search for agent session models, lifecycle state helpers, and existing telemetry patterns first
   - Create or refactor a narrow service that merges local running-agent sessions and cloud-agent session progress into one normalized projection for the UI
   - Preserve current tree-view behavior while exposing the same normalized data to a webview-friendly bridge so the prototype has one source of truth
   - Include lifecycle metadata that is already available or derivable now: session id, source, title, agent name, state, timestamps, worktree status, last visible activity, and blocking status
   - Note: completed on 2026-04-30 by extending `src/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.ts` to merge persisted and live agent-chat sessions, derive transcript-backed titles/activity, and normalize provider display names for cloud sessions without changing existing tree providers.
   - Validation: re-verified on 2026-04-30 with `tests/unit/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.test.ts` and `npm run check`; the check required clearing a disposable oversized `.entire/tmp` session artifact before passing.
 
 - [x] Build the first orchestration prototype view that visibly works inside the extension:
   - Reuse the existing page registry, panel, and bridge patterns before introducing new routing or state libraries
   - Add a dedicated orchestration view or panel that renders the normalized session projection with clear columns or cards for Active, Waiting, Completed, and Failed work
   - Ensure the prototype allows at minimum: open session, refresh session state, inspect current status, and navigate back to the existing agent-chat or cloud-agent experience
   - Keep the UI intentionally simple but visually compelling so the user can see live orchestration progress as soon as the phase is complete
   - Note: completed on 2026-04-30 by wiring the existing orchestration webview provider into the extension bootstrap, adding a simple four-lane card UI in `ui/src/features/orchestration/index.tsx`, and fixing cloud-session navigation so the prototype can jump back to the Cloud Agents surface instead of only refreshing state.
   - Validation: re-verified on 2026-04-30 with `tests/unit/webview/cloud-agent-progress-view.test.tsx` and `npm run check`.
 
-- [ ] Connect orchestration events, telemetry, and empty states so the prototype behaves like a real product surface:
+- [x] Connect orchestration events, telemetry, and empty states so the prototype behaves like a real product surface:
   - Reuse existing event emitters, output channels, and telemetry helpers before adding new instrumentation paths
   - Make tree updates and orchestration view updates respond to session-store, registry, and cloud-provider changes without manual reloads where possible
   - Add actionable empty, loading, and degraded states that explain whether there are no sessions, missing providers, or failed status reads
   - Write implementation notes in `docs/architecture/orchestration/orchestration-ui-contract.md` with YAML front matter and wiki-links to `[[Running-Agents-Prototype]]`
+  - Note: completed on 2026-04-30 by wiring orchestration refreshes to existing `AgentSessionStorage` and `ProviderRegistry` change events, replacing the prototype's timer-based provider refresh path, and upgrading the orchestration webview with actionable no-session, missing-provider, and degraded-state messaging.
+  - Validation: verified on 2026-04-30 with `tests/unit/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.test.ts`, `tests/unit/webview/cloud-agent-progress-view.test.tsx`, and `npm run check`.
 
 - [ ] Write test coverage for the orchestration prototype behavior:
   - Add or update unit tests for the normalized orchestration read model, lifecycle grouping, and any new state selectors or adapters
   - Add webview or provider-focused tests that verify the prototype renders meaningful empty, active, and failed states from representative session data
   - Prefer extending existing test suites around providers and stores before creating entirely new harnesses
 
 - [ ] Run the orchestration quality gates and fix failures until the prototype is working end-to-end:
   - Run the relevant targeted tests for orchestration changes first
   - Run `npm run check`
   - Run the full affected test command if targeted tests expose regressions that require broader validation
   - Verify the extension can surface the new orchestration prototype without breaking the existing Running Agents and Cloud Agents views

 # Orchestration UI Contract
 
 See also: [[Running-Agents-Prototype]]
 
+## Purpose
+
+This contract defines the smallest bridge between the extension host and the phase 01 orchestration webview. It reuses the existing agent-chat telemetry helper, cloud-agent polling/runtime, and page-registry/webview patterns instead of creating a second orchestration stack.
+
 ## Inbound Messages
 
-- `orchestration/ready`: webview asks the extension for the current snapshot.
-- `orchestration/refresh`: webview asks the extension to refresh cloud polling and republish the snapshot.
-- `orchestration/open-session`: webview asks the extension to open the selected session in the best existing surface.
-- `orchestration/open-existing-surface`: webview asks the extension to reveal the original surface for a session source.
-- `orchestration/open-external`: webview asks the extension to open an external provider URL.
+- `orchestration/ready`: ask the extension for the current normalized snapshot.
+- `orchestration/refresh`: refresh cloud state through the existing cloud-agent command path, then republish the orchestration snapshot.
+- `orchestration/open-session`: open the selected session in the best existing surface.
+- `orchestration/open-existing-surface`: reveal the original Agent Chat or Cloud Agents experience for the requested source.
+- `orchestration/open-external`: open an external provider URL for cloud sessions.
 
 ## Outbound Messages
 
-- `orchestration/snapshot`: extension publishes the normalized orchestration snapshot.
+- `orchestration/snapshot`: publish the normalized orchestration snapshot to the webview.
 
 ## Snapshot Shape
 
 - `sessions[]`: normalized session cards with `id`, `source`, `sourceSessionId`, `title`, `agentName`, `state`, `bucket`, timestamps, blocking metadata, worktree metadata, and open-session routing hints.
 - `activeProvider`: current cloud provider metadata when present.
-- `generatedAt`: snapshot creation timestamp.
-- `degradedReasons[]`: user-facing degraded-state explanations.
+- `generatedAt`: snapshot creation timestamp used by the UI header.
+- `degradedReasons[]`: user-facing degraded-state explanations for missing providers, unavailable storage, or failed status reads.
 
-## Empty and Degraded States
+## Live Update Contract
 
-- Empty state explains that no local or cloud sessions exist yet.
-- Degraded state explains when cloud storage or provider wiring is unavailable.
-- Waiting sessions are surfaced as blocked work so they do not disappear inside the active list.
+- Agent-chat changes flow through the existing session-store manifest event and live registry event.
+- Cloud session changes flow through `AgentSessionStorage.onDidChange`, which now fires after create, update, delete, and read-only transitions.
+- Cloud provider changes flow through `ProviderRegistry.onDidChange`, which now fires after registration and active-provider changes.
+- The orchestration read model subscribes to those existing surfaces so the tree views and orchestration view stay in sync without manual reload loops.
+
+## Empty, Loading, and Degraded States
+
+- Loading state appears until the first `orchestration/snapshot` arrives.
+- Empty state explains there are no local or cloud sessions yet and offers a jump back to an existing surface.
+- Missing-provider empty states explain whether no providers are registered or no active provider is selected.
+- Degraded state explains when cloud storage is unavailable or when local/cloud status reads fail, and points the user toward refresh plus the output channel.
+- Waiting sessions are surfaced as blocked work so they do not disappear inside the active lane.
+
+## Reused vs New Modules
+
+- Reused: [[Running-Agents-Prototype]], `src/features/agent-chat/telemetry.ts`, `src/features/cloud-agents/agent-polling-service.ts`, `src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts`, and the shared webview page registry.
+- New or narrowed for phase 01: `src/providers/orchestration-view-provider.ts`, `src/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.ts`, and the orchestration React view.

"# Context

Your name is **Dori**, a Maestro-managed AI agent. You are executing tasks from a **Playbook** — a collection of Auto Run documents. Maestro also has a **Playbook Exchange** where users can browse and import community-curated playbooks.

- **Agent Path:** /Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode
- **Git Branch:** 018-agent-chat-panel
- **Auto Run Folder:** /Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs
- **Loop Iteration:** 00001
- **Working Folder for Temporary Files:** /Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs/Working

If you need to create the working folder, do so.

---

## CRITICAL: Response Format Requirement

**Your response MUST begin with a specific, actionable synopsis of what you accomplished.**

- GOOD examples: \"Added pagination to the user list component\", \"Fixed authentication timeout bug in login.ts\", \"Refactored database queries to use prepared statements\"
- BAD examples: \"The task is complete\", \"Task completed successfully\", \"Done\", \"Finished the task\"

The synopsis is displayed in the History panel and must describe the actual work done, not just that work was done.

---

## Structured Output Artifacts

When creating documentation, research notes, reports, or any knowledge artifacts (not source code), use **structured Markdown** by default:

### YAML Front Matter

```yaml
---
type: research | note | report | analysis | reference
title: Descriptive Title
created: YYYY-MM-DD
tags:
  - relevant-tag
related:
  - '[[Other-Document]]'
---
```

### Wiki-Link Cross-References

Use `[[Document-Name]]` syntax to connect related documents. This enables graph exploration in Maestro's DocGraph viewer and tools like Obsidian.

### Folder Organization

Organize artifacts in logical folders by entity type or domain:

```
docs/
├── research/
│   ├── topic-a.md
│   └── topic-b.md
├── architecture/
│   └── system-design.md
└── decisions/
    └── adr-001-choice.md
```

**When to apply:** Research findings, competitive analysis, architecture decisions, technical specs, meeting notes, reference docs, glossaries.

**When NOT to apply:** Source code files, config files (JSON/YAML), generated assets, temporary files.

## Instructions

1. Project Orientation
   Begin by reviewing CLAUDE.md (when available) in this folder to understand the project's structure, conventions, and workflow expectations.

2. Task Selection
   Process the FIRST unchecked task (- [ ]) from top to bottom. Note that there may be relevant images associated with the task. If there are, analyze them, and include in your final synopsis back how many images you analyzed in preparation for solving the task.

   IMPORTANT: You will only work on this single task. If the task description contains multiple steps or subtasks (e.g., bullet points, numbered lists, or semicolon-separated items), treat them as one cohesive unit—they were intentionally grouped by the document author to share context. Complete all parts before marking done. Do not move on to the next major checkbox item.

3. Task Evaluation
   - Fully understand the task and inspect the relevant code.
   - Identify all subtasks within the current checkbox item.
   - There will be future runs to take care of other checkbox items.

4. Task Implementation
   - **Before creating new code**, search for existing implementations, utilities, helpers, or patterns in the codebase that can be reused or extended. Avoid duplicating functionality that already exists.
   - Implement the task according to the project's established style, architecture, and coding norms.
   - Ensure that test cases are created, and that they pass.
   - Ensure you haven't broken any existing test cases.

5. Completion + Reporting
   - Mark the task as completed by changing \"- [ ]\" to \"- [x]\".
   - Begin your response with the specific synopsis (see \"Response Format Requirement\" above).
   - Follow with any relevant details about:
     - Implementation approach or key decisions made
     - Why the task was intentionally skipped (if applicable)
     - If implementation failed, explain the failure and do NOT check off the item.

6. Version Control
   For any code or documentation changes, if we're in a Github repo:
   - Commit using a descriptive message prefixed with \"MAESTRO: \".
   - Push to GitHub.
   - Update CLAUDE.md, README.md, or any other top-level documentation if appropriate.

7. Exit Immediately
   After completing (or skipping) your task, EXIT. Do not proceed to additional tasks—another agent instance will handle them. If there are no remaining open tasks, exit immediately and state that there is nothing left to do.

---

## Tasks

Process tasks from this document:

/Users/t798157/Projects/lab/gatomia-vscode/Auto Run Docs/Initiation/2026-04-30-gatomia-orchestration-playbook/GATOMIA-ORCHESTRATION-01.md

Check off tasks and add any relevant notes around the completion directly within that document.
"

It seems I’ll need to commit and push changes since the user specifically requests it. I'll check if there are any associated images linked in the markdown. Additionally, I should do commentary updates before significant work and perform a search in the working folder. Let's get started with that!
Reviewing the playbook and local guidance first so I can implement only the first open task and keep the repo workflow consistent.

61: export interface OrchestrationReadModelOptions {

63: 	readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
64: 	readonly cloudSessionStorage?: AgentSessionStorage;
65: 	readonly cloudProviderRegistry?: ProviderRegistry;
66: 	readonly agentChatStoreChangeEvent: Event<unknown>;
67: 	readonly onCloudSessionsChanged?: (listener: () => void) => {
68: 		dispose(): void;
69: 	};

71: 		dispose(): void;

76: export class OrchestrationReadModel {
77: 	private readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;
78: 	private readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
79: 	private readonly cloudSessionStorage?: AgentSessionStorage;
80: 	private readonly cloudProviderRegistry?: ProviderRegistry;
81: 	private readonly now: () => number;
82: 	private readonly subscriptions: Array<{ dispose(): void }> = [];
83: 	private readonly listeners = new Set<() => void>();
84: 
85: 	constructor(options: OrchestrationReadModelOptions) {
86: 		this.store = options.store;
87: 		this.registry = options.registry;
88: 		this.cloudSessionStorage = options.cloudSessionStorage;
89: 		this.cloudProviderRegistry = options.cloudProviderRegistry;
90: 		this.now = options.now ?? Date.now;
91: 
92: 		this.subscriptions.push(
93: 			options.agentChatStoreChangeEvent(() => {
94: 				this.emitChanged();
95: 			})
96: 		);
97: 		this.subscriptions.push(
98: 			this.registry.onDidChange(() => {
99: 				this.emitChanged();
100: 			})
101: 		);
102: 		if (options.onCloudSessionsChanged) {
103: 			this.subscriptions.push(
104: 				options.onCloudSessionsChanged(() => {
105: 					this.emitChanged();
106: 				})
107: 			);
108: 		}
109: 		if (options.onCloudProviderChanged) {
110: 			this.subscriptions.push(
111: 				options.onCloudProviderChanged(() => {
112: 					this.emitChanged();
113: 				})
114: 			);
115: 		}
116: 	}
117: 
118: 	onDidChange(listener: () => void): { dispose(): void } {
119: 		this.listeners.add(listener);
120: 		return {
121: 			dispose: () => {
122: 				this.listeners.delete(listener);
123: 			},
124: 		};
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	async snapshot(): Promise<OrchestrationSnapshot> {
128: 		const sessions: OrchestrationSessionProjection[] = [];
129: 		const degradedReasons: string[] = [];
130: 
131: 		const [agentChatSessions, cloudSessions] = await Promise.all([
132: 			this.collectAgentChatSessions(),
133: 			this.collectCloudSessions(degradedReasons),
134: 		]);
135: 
136: 		sessions.push(...agentChatSessions, ...cloudSessions);
137: 		sessions.sort(compareSessions);
138: 
139: 		const activeProvider = this.cloudProviderRegistry?.getActive();
140: 
141: 		return {
142: 			sessions,
143: 			activeProvider: activeProvider
144: 				? {
145: 						id: activeProvider.metadata.id,
146: 						displayName: activeProvider.metadata.displayName,
147: 					}
148: 				: undefined,
149: 			generatedAt: this.now(),
150: 			degradedReasons,
151: 		};
152: 	}
153: 
154: 	dispose(): void {
155: 		for (const subscription of this.subscriptions) {
156: 			try {
157: 				subscription.dispose();
158: 			} catch {
159: 				// best-effort
160: 			}
161: 		}
162: 		this.subscriptions.length = 0;
163: 		this.listeners.clear();
164: 	}
165: 
166: 	private emitChanged(): void {
167: 		for (const listener of [...this.listeners]) {
168: 			try {
169: 				listener();
170: 			} catch {
171: 				// best-effort
172: 			}
173: 		}
174: 	}
175: 
176: 	private async collectAgentChatSessions(): Promise<
177: 		OrchestrationSessionProjection[]
178: 	> {
179: 		const [active, recent] = await Promise.all([
180: 			this.store.listActive(),
181: 			this.store.listRecent(),
182: 		]);
183: 		const byId = new Map<string, AgentChatSession>();
184: 		for (const session of [...active, ...recent]) {
185: 			byId.set(session.id, session);
186: 		}
187: 		for (const session of [
188: 			...this.registry.listActive(),
189: 			...this.registry.listRecent(),
190: 		]) {
191: 			byId.set(session.id, session);
192: 		}
193: 		return [...byId.values()].map((session) =>
194: 			toAgentChatProjection(session, this.readTranscript(session.id))
195: 		);
196: 	}
197: 
198: 	private async collectCloudSessions(
199: 		degradedReasons: string[]
200: 	): Promise<OrchestrationSessionProjection[]> {
201: 		if (!this.cloudSessionStorage) {
202: 			degradedReasons.push("Cloud agent session storage is unavailable.");
203: 			return [];
204: 		}
205: 		const sessions = await this.cloudSessionStorage.getAll();
206: 		return sessions.map((session) =>
207: 			toCloudProjection(session, this.cloudProviderRegistry)
208: 		);
209: 	}
210: 
211: 	private readTranscript(sessionId: string): ChatMessage[] {
212: 		const workspaceState = (
213: 			this.store as unknown as {
214: 				workspaceState?: { get<T>(key: string): T | undefined };
215: 			}
216: 		).workspaceState;
217: 		if (!workspaceState) {
218: 			return [];
219: 		}
220: 		const raw = workspaceState.get<{ messages: ChatMessage[] }>(
221: 			transcriptKeyFor(sessionId)
222: 		);
223: 		return raw?.messages ?? [];
224: 	}
225: }
226: 
227: function toAgentChatProjection(
228: 	session: AgentChatSession,
229: 	transcript: readonly ChatMessage[]
230: ): OrchestrationSessionProjection {
231: 	const title = deriveAgentChatTitle(session, transcript);
232: 	const lastVisibleActivityAt = deriveLastVisibleActivityAt(
233: 		session,
234: 		transcript
235: 	);
236: 	return {
237: 		id: `agent-chat:${session.id}`,
238: 		source: "agent-chat",
239: 		sourceSessionId: session.id,
240: 		title,
241: 		agentName: session.agentDisplayName,
242: 		state: session.lifecycleState,
243: 		bucket: bucketForAgentChat(session.lifecycleState),
244: 		createdAt: session.createdAt,
245: 		updatedAt: session.updatedAt,
246: 		endedAt: session.endedAt,
247: 		lastVisibleActivityAt,
248: 		isBlocked: session.lifecycleState === "waiting-for-input",
249: 		worktreeStatus: session.worktree?.status,
250: 		executionTargetLabel: executionTargetLabel(session.executionTarget.kind),
251: 		externalUrl: session.cloud?.externalUrl,
252: 		cloudProviderId: session.cloud?.providerId,
253: 		openSessionCommand: { kind: "agent-chat", sessionId: session.id },
254: 	};
255: }
256: 
257: function toCloudProjection(
258: 	session: AgentSession,
259: 	providerRegistry: ProviderRegistry | undefined
260: ): OrchestrationSessionProjection {
261: 	const firstTask = session.tasks[0];
262: 	const title = firstTask
263: 		? `${firstTask.specTaskId}: ${firstTask.title}`
264: 		: session.specPath;
265: 	const providerDisplayName =
266: 		providerRegistry?.get(session.providerId)?.metadata.displayName ??
267: 		session.providerId;
268: 	const lastVisibleActivityAt = firstTask?.startedAt ?? session.updatedAt;
269: 	return {
270: 		id: `cloud-agent:${session.localId}`,
271: 		source: "cloud-agent",
272: 		sourceSessionId: session.localId,
273: 		title,
274: 		agentName: providerDisplayName,
275: 		state: session.status,
276: 		bucket: bucketForCloud(session.status),
277: 		createdAt: session.createdAt,
278: 		updatedAt: session.updatedAt,
279: 		endedAt: session.completedAt,
280: 		lastVisibleActivityAt,
281: 		isBlocked: session.status === SessionStatus.BLOCKED,
282: 		worktreeStatus: session.isReadOnly ? "read-only" : undefined,
283: 		executionTargetLabel: "Cloud",
284: 		externalUrl: session.externalUrl,
285: 		cloudProviderId: session.providerId,
286: 		openSessionCommand: {
287: 			kind: "cloud-agent",
288: 			localId: session.localId,
289: 			externalUrl: session.externalUrl,
290: 		},
291: 	};
292: }
293: 
294: function deriveAgentChatTitle(
295: 	session: AgentChatSession,
296: 	transcript: readonly ChatMessage[]
297: ): string {
298: 	const promptTitle = firstUserMessageText(transcript);
299: 	if (promptTitle) {
300: 		return promptTitle;
301: 	}
302: 	if (session.selectedModeId) {
303: 		return `${session.agentDisplayName} (${session.selectedModeId})`;
304: 	}
305: 	return session.agentDisplayName;
306: }
307: 
308: function deriveLastVisibleActivityAt(
309: 	session: AgentChatSession,
310: 	transcript: readonly ChatMessage[]
311: ): number {
312: 	let latest = session.updatedAt;
313: 	for (const message of transcript) {
314: 		if (message.timestamp > latest) {
315: 			latest = message.timestamp;
316: 		}
317: 	}
318: 	return latest;
319: }
320: 
321: function firstUserMessageText(
322: 	transcript: readonly ChatMessage[]
323: ): string | undefined {
324: 	for (const message of transcript) {
325: 		if (message.role !== "user") {
326: 			continue;
327: 		}
328: 		const content = normalizePromptTitle(message);
329: 		if (content) {
330: 			return content;
331: 		}
332: 	}
333: 	return;
334: }
335: 
336: function normalizePromptTitle(message: UserChatMessage): string | undefined {
337: 	const text = message.content.trim().replace(/\s+/g, " ");
338: 	return text.length > 0 ? text : undefined;
339: }
340: 
341: function bucketForAgentChat(
342: 	state: SessionLifecycleState
343: ): OrchestrationSessionBucket {
344: 	if (state === "waiting-for-input") {
345: 		return "waiting";
346: 	}
347: 	if (state === "completed") {
348: 		return "completed";
349: 	}
350: 	if (
351: 		state === "failed" ||
352: 		state === "cancelled" ||
353: 		state === "ended-by-shutdown"
354: 	) {
355: 		return "failed";
356: 	}
357: 	return "active";
358: }
359: 
360: function bucketForCloud(
361: 	status: AgentSession["status"]
362: ): OrchestrationSessionBucket {
363: 	if (status === SessionStatus.BLOCKED) {
364: 		return "waiting";
365: 	}
366: 	if (status === SessionStatus.COMPLETED) {
367: 		return "completed";
368: 	}
369: 	if (status === SessionStatus.FAILED || status === SessionStatus.CANCELLED) {
370: 		return "failed";
371: 	}
372: 	return "active";
373: }
374: 
375: function executionTargetLabel(kind: "local" | "worktree" | "cloud"): string {
376: 	if (kind === "worktree") {
377: 		return "Worktree";
378: 	}
379: 	if (kind === "cloud") {
380: 		return "Cloud";
381: 	}
382: 	return "Local";
383: }
384: 
385: function compareSessions(
386: 	a: OrchestrationSessionProjection,
387: 	b: OrchestrationSessionProjection
388: ): number {
389: 	if (a.bucket !== b.bucket) {
390: 		return bucketRank(a.bucket) - bucketRank(b.bucket);
391: 	}
392: 	return b.lastVisibleActivityAt - a.lastVisibleActivityAt;
393: }
394: 
395: function bucketRank(bucket: OrchestrationSessionBucket): number {
396: 	if (bucket === "active") {
397: 		return 0;
398: 	}
399: 	if (bucket === "waiting") {
400: 		return 1;
401: 	}
402: 	if (bucket === "completed") {
403: 		return 2;
404: 	}
405: 	return 3;
406: }

1: ---

4: author: OpenCode

7: 
8: # Orchestration UI Contract
9: 
10: See also: [[Running-Agents-Prototype]]
11: 
12: ## Inbound Messages
13: 
14: - `orchestration/ready`: webview asks the extension for the current snapshot.
15: - `orchestration/refresh`: webview asks the extension to refresh cloud polling and republish the snapshot.
16: - `orchestration/open-session`: webview asks the extension to open the selected session in the best existing surface.
17: - `orchestration/open-existing-surface`: webview asks the extension to reveal the original surface for a session source.
18: - `orchestration/open-external`: webview asks the extension to open an external provider URL.
19: 
20: ## Outbound Messages
21: 
22: - `orchestration/snapshot`: extension publishes the normalized orchestration snapshot.
23: 
24: ## Snapshot Shape
25: 
26: - `sessions[]`: normalized session cards with `id`, `source`, `sourceSessionId`, `title`, `agentName`, `state`, `bucket`, timestamps, blocking metadata, worktree metadata, and open-session routing hints.
27: - `activeProvider`: current cloud provider metadata when present.
28: - `generatedAt`: snapshot creation timestamp.
29: - `degradedReasons[]`: user-facing degraded-state explanations.
30: 
31: ## Empty and Degraded States
32: 
33: - Empty state explains that no local or cloud sessions exist yet.
34: - Degraded state explains when cloud storage or provider wiring is unavailable.
35: - Waiting sessions are surfaced as blocked work so they do not disappear inside the active list.

(End of file - total 35 lines)

# Orchestration UI Contract

See also: [[Running-Agents-Prototype]]

## Inbound Messages

- `orchestration/ready`: webview asks the extension for the current snapshot.
- `orchestration/refresh`: webview asks the extension to refresh cloud polling and republish the snapshot.
- `orchestration/open-session`: webview asks the extension to open the selected session in the best existing surface.
- `orchestration/open-existing-surface`: webview asks the extension to reveal the original surface for a session source.
- `orchestration/open-external`: webview asks the extension to open an external provider URL.

1: /**
2:  * Cloud Agent Progress Provider
3:  *
4:  * Provider-agnostic tree view data provider for the Cloud Agents sidebar.
5:  * Shows sessions with task details, external links, and status icons.
6:  *
7:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/plan.md
8:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/ui-events.md
9:  */

12: 	EventEmitter,

27: // ============================================================================

29: /**
30:  * Wrapper that carries session/task data through the tree.
31:  */
32: class CloudAgentTreeItem extends TreeItem {
33: 	readonly session: AgentSession | undefined;
34: 	readonly agentTask: AgentTask | undefined;
35: 
36: 	constructor(
37: 		label: string,
38: 		collapsibleState: TreeItemCollapsibleState,
39: 		session?: AgentSession,
40: 		agentTask?: AgentTask
41: 	) {
42: 		super(label, collapsibleState);
43: 		this.session = session;
44: 		this.agentTask = agentTask;
45: 	}
46: }

48: // ============================================================================
49: // CloudAgentProgressProvider
50: // ============================================================================
51: 
52: /**
53:  * Tree view data provider for the Cloud Agents sidebar.
54:  * Shows active provider sessions with tasks, external links, and PR info.
55:  *
56:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/ui-events.md
57:  */
58: export class CloudAgentProgressProvider
59: 	implements TreeDataProvider<CloudAgentTreeItem>
60: {
61: 	private readonly registry: ProviderRegistry;
62: 	private readonly sessionStorage: AgentSessionStorage | undefined;
63: 	private readonly _onDidChangeTreeData = new EventEmitter<
64: 		CloudAgentTreeItem | undefined | null
65: 	>();
66: 	readonly onDidChangeTreeData: Event<CloudAgentTreeItem | undefined | null> =
67: 		this._onDidChangeTreeData.event;
68: 
69: 	constructor(
70: 		registry: ProviderRegistry,
71: 		sessionStorage?: AgentSessionStorage
72: 	) {
73: 		this.registry = registry;
74: 		this.sessionStorage = sessionStorage;
75: 	}
76: 
77: 	/**
78: 	 * Refresh the tree view.
79: 	 */
80: 	refresh(): void {
81: 		this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(null);
82: 	}
83: 
84: 	/**
85: 	 * Update the context key for welcome content visibility.
86: 	 */
87: 	async updateContextKeys(): Promise<void> {
88: 		const active = this.registry.getActive();
89: 		await commands.executeCommand(
90: 			"setContext",
91: 			"gatomia.cloudAgent.hasProvider",
92: 			active !== undefined
93: 		);
94: 		if (active) {
95: 			await commands.executeCommand(
96: 				"setContext",
97: 				"gatomia.cloudAgent.activeProvider",
98: 				active.metadata.id
99: 			);
100: 		}
101: 	}
102: 
103: 	getTreeItem(element: CloudAgentTreeItem): CloudAgentTreeItem {
104: 		return element;
105: 	}
106: 
107: 	async getChildren(
108: 		element?: CloudAgentTreeItem
109: 	): Promise<CloudAgentTreeItem[]> {
110: 		if (element?.session && !element.agentTask) {
111: 			return this.getSessionChildren(element.session);
112: 		}
113: 
114: 		const active = this.registry.getActive();
115: 		if (!active) {
116: 			return [];
117: 		}
118: 
119: 		if (!this.sessionStorage) {
120: 			return [];
121: 		}
122: 
123: 		const allSessions = await this.sessionStorage.getAll();
124: 		if (allSessions.length === 0) {
125: 			const item = new CloudAgentTreeItem(
126: 				"No sessions",
127: 				TreeItemCollapsibleState.None
128: 			);
129: 			item.description = active.metadata.displayName;
130: 			return [item];
131: 		}
132: 
133: 		return allSessions.map((s) =>
134: 			this.createSessionItem(s, active.metadata.id)
135: 		);
136: 	}
137: 
138: 	private createSessionItem(
139: 		session: AgentSession,
140: 		activeProviderId: string
141: 	): CloudAgentTreeItem {
142: 		const firstTask = session.tasks[0];
143: 		const label = firstTask
144: 			? `${firstTask.specTaskId}: ${firstTask.title}`
145: 			: session.localId;
146: 
147: 		const hasTasks =
148: 			session.tasks.length > 0 || session.pullRequests.length > 0;
149: 		const item = new CloudAgentTreeItem(
150: 			label,
151: 			hasTasks
152: 				? TreeItemCollapsibleState.Collapsed
153: 				: TreeItemCollapsibleState.None,
154: 			session
155: 		);
156: 
157: 		item.description = session.isReadOnly
158: 			? `${session.status} (read-only)`
159: 			: session.status;
160: 
161: 		const tooltipLines = [
162: 			`Status: ${session.status}`,
163: 			`Provider: ${session.providerId}`,
164: 			`Branch: ${session.branch}`,
165: 			`Spec: ${session.specPath}`,
166: 		];
167: 		if (session.externalUrl) {
168: 			tooltipLines.push(`URL: ${session.externalUrl}`);
169: 		}
170: 		if (session.tasks.length > 1) {
171: 			tooltipLines.push(`Tasks: ${session.tasks.length}`);
172: 		}
173: 		if (session.pullRequests.length > 0) {
174: 			tooltipLines.push(`PRs: ${session.pullRequests.length}`);
175: 		}
176: 		item.tooltip = tooltipLines.join("\n");
177: 
178: 		item.iconPath = this.getStatusIcon(session.status);
179: 		item.contextValue =
180: 			session.providerId === activeProviderId && !session.isReadOnly
181: 				? `session.${session.status}`
182: 				: "session.readonly";
183: 
184: 		if (session.externalUrl) {
185: 			item.command = {
186: 				command: "vscode.open",
187: 				title: "Open in Browser",
188: 				arguments: [Uri.parse(session.externalUrl)],
189: 			};
190: 		}
191: 
192: 		return item;
193: 	}
194: 
195: 	private getSessionChildren(session: AgentSession): CloudAgentTreeItem[] {
196: 		const children: CloudAgentTreeItem[] = [];
197: 
198: 		for (const task of session.tasks) {
199: 			const taskItem = new CloudAgentTreeItem(
200: 				`${task.specTaskId}: ${task.title}`,
201: 				TreeItemCollapsibleState.None,
202: 				session,
203: 				task
204: 			);
205: 			taskItem.description = task.status;
206: 			taskItem.iconPath = this.getTaskStatusIcon(task.status);
207: 			taskItem.tooltip = [
208: 				`Task: ${task.title}`,
209: 				`Status: ${task.status}`,
210: 				`Priority: ${task.priority}`,
211: 			].join("\n");
212: 			children.push(taskItem);
213: 		}
214: 
215: 		for (const pr of session.pullRequests) {
216: 			const prItem = new CloudAgentTreeItem(
217: 				`PR: ${pr.branch}`,
218: 				TreeItemCollapsibleState.None,
219: 				session
220: 			);
221: 			prItem.description = pr.state ?? "open";
222: 			prItem.iconPath = new ThemeIcon("git-pull-request");
223: 			prItem.tooltip = pr.url;
224: 			prItem.command = {
225: 				command: "vscode.open",
226: 				title: "Open Pull Request",
227: 				arguments: [Uri.parse(pr.url)],
228: 			};
229: 			children.push(prItem);
230: 		}
231: 
232: 		if (session.externalUrl) {
233: 			const linkItem = new CloudAgentTreeItem(
234: 				"Open in Provider",
235: 				TreeItemCollapsibleState.None,
236: 				session
237: 			);
238: 			linkItem.iconPath = new ThemeIcon("link-external");
239: 			linkItem.tooltip = session.externalUrl;
240: 			linkItem.command = {
241: 				command: "vscode.open",
242: 				title: "Open in Browser",
243: 				arguments: [Uri.parse(session.externalUrl)],
244: 			};
245: 			children.push(linkItem);
246: 		}
247: 
248: 		return children;
249: 	}
250: 
251: 	private getStatusIcon(status: string): ThemeIcon {
252: 		switch (status) {
253: 			case "running":
254: 				return new ThemeIcon("sync~spin");
255: 			case "completed":
256: 				return new ThemeIcon("check");
257: 			case "failed":
258: 				return new ThemeIcon("error");
259: 			case "blocked":
260: 				return new ThemeIcon("warning");
261: 			case "cancelled":
262: 				return new ThemeIcon("circle-slash");
263: 			default:
264: 				return new ThemeIcon("circle-outline");
265: 		}
266: 	}
267: 
268: 	private getTaskStatusIcon(status: string): ThemeIcon {
269: 		switch (status) {
270: 			case "in_progress":
271: 				return new ThemeIcon("sync~spin");
272: 			case "completed":

274: 			case "failed":
275: 				return new ThemeIcon("error");
276: 			case "skipped":
277: 				return new ThemeIcon("circle-slash");
278: 			default:
279: 				return new ThemeIcon("circle-outline");
280: 		}
281: 	}
282: 
283: 	dispose(): void {
284: 		this._onDidChangeTreeData.dispose();
285: 	}
286: }

50: const MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES = 3;
51: const PR_GRACE_PERIOD_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000;
52: const PR_UNKNOWN_STATE_GRACE_PERIOD_MS = 60 * 60 * 1000;
53: 
54: const TERMINAL_PR_STATES = new Set(["merged", "closed"]);
55: 
56: // ============================================================================
57: // AgentPollingService
58: // ============================================================================
59: 
60: /**
61:  * Polling orchestration that delegates to the active provider.
62:  * Includes retry behavior and credential-expiry detection (FR-020).
63:  *
64:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/provider-interface.md
65:  */
66: export class AgentPollingService {
67: 	private readonly registry: ProviderRegistry;
68: 	private readonly sessionStorage: AgentSessionStorage;
69: 	private intervalId: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | undefined;
70: 	private consecutiveFailures = 0;
71: 	private readonly callbacks = {
72: 		onCredentialExpiry: undefined as (() => void) | undefined,
73: 		onSessionCompleted: undefined as
74: 			| ((localId: string, specPath: string) => void)
75: 			| undefined,
76: 		onError: undefined as ((message: string) => void) | undefined,
77: 		onUpdated: undefined as (() => void) | undefined,
78: 	};
79: 
80: 	/**
81: 	 * Subscribers for per-session update events (T017, spec 018 bridge).
82: 	 * Additive to the existing single-callback `onUpdated` hook — both fire on
83: 	 * the same commit path.
84: 	 */
85: 	private readonly sessionUpdatedListeners =
86: 		new Set<AgentSessionUpdatedListener>();
87: 
88: 	/**
89: 	 * Whether interval-based polling is currently running.
90: 	 */
91: 	get isRunning(): boolean {
92: 		return this.intervalId !== undefined;
93: 	}
94: 
95: 	constructor(registry: ProviderRegistry, sessionStorage: AgentSessionStorage) {
96: 		this.registry = registry;
97: 		this.sessionStorage = sessionStorage;
98: 	}
99: 
100: 	/**
101: 	 * Register a callback for credential-expiry events (FR-020).
102: 	 */
103: 	setOnCredentialExpiry(handler: () => void): void {
104: 		this.callbacks.onCredentialExpiry = handler;
105: 	}
106: 
107: 	/**
108: 	 * Register a callback for provider errors (SC-006).
109: 	 * Called with a user-facing error message when an API failure is detected.
110: 	 */
111: 	setOnError(handler: (message: string) => void): void {
112: 		this.callbacks.onError = handler;
113: 	}
114: 
115: 	/**
116: 	 * Register a callback fired after each poll cycle that applied updates.
117: 	 * Used to refresh the tree view UI.
118: 	 */
119: 	setOnUpdated(handler: () => void): void {
120: 		this.callbacks.onUpdated = handler;
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	/**
124: 	 * Register a callback for session completion events (FR-017).
125: 	 * Called with (localId, specPath) when a session reaches a terminal state.
126: 	 */
127: 	setOnSessionCompleted(
128: 		handler: (localId: string, specPath: string) => void
129: 	): void {
130: 		this.callbacks.onSessionCompleted = handler;
131: 	}
132: 
133: 	/**
134: 	 * Subscribe to per-session update events (T017, spec 018 bridge). Fires
135: 	 * once per mutated session after each poll cycle commits.
136: 	 *
137: 	 * Returns a {@link Disposable} that removes the subscription without
138: 	 * affecting other listeners.
139: 	 */
140: 	onSessionUpdated(listener: AgentSessionUpdatedListener): Disposable {
141: 		this.sessionUpdatedListeners.add(listener);
142: 		return {
143: 			dispose: () => {
144: 				this.sessionUpdatedListeners.delete(listener);
145: 			},
146: 		};
147: 	}
148: 
149: 	/**
150: 	 * Execute a single poll cycle with retry and credential-expiry detection.
151: 	 * @param force - When true, poll all non-read-only sessions regardless of status or grace period.
152: 	 *               Use for user-initiated refresh to always query the provider.
153: 	 */
154: 	async pollOnce(force = false): Promise<void> {
155: 		const provider = this.registry.getActive();
156: 		if (!provider) {
157: 			logDebug("No active provider, skipping poll");
158: 			return;
159: 		}
160: 
161: 		try {
162: 			const sessions = force
163: 				? await this.getAllPollableSessions()
164: 				: await this.getSessionsToPoll();
165: 			if (sessions.length === 0) {
166: 				return;
167: 			}
168: 
169: 			const updates = await provider.pollSessions(sessions);
170: 			for (const update of updates) {
171: 				await this.applyUpdate(update);
172: 			}
173: 
174: 			this.consecutiveFailures = 0;
175: 			if (updates.length > 0) {
176: 				logDebug(`Applied ${updates.length} session update(s)`);
177: 				this.callbacks.onUpdated?.();
178: 			}
179: 		} catch (error) {
180: 			this.handlePollError(error);
181: 		}
182: 	}
183: 
184: 	/**
185: 	 * Start interval-based polling.
186: 	 * @param intervalMs - Polling interval in milliseconds
187: 	 */
188: 	start(intervalMs: number): void {
189: 		if (this.intervalId) {
190: 			return;
191: 		}
192: 		this.consecutiveFailures = 0;
193: 		this.intervalId = setInterval(() => {
194: 			this.pollOnce().catch((error) => {
195: 				logError("Interval poll failed", error);
196: 			});
197: 		}, intervalMs);
198: 		logDebug(`Polling started with ${intervalMs}ms interval`);
199: 	}
200: 
201: 	/**
202: 	 * Stop interval-based polling.
203: 	 */
204: 	stop(): void {
205: 		if (this.intervalId) {
206: 			clearInterval(this.intervalId);
207: 			this.intervalId = undefined;
208: 			logDebug("Polling stopped");
209: 		}
210: 	}
211: 
212: 	private async getAllPollableSessions(): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
213: 		const all = await this.sessionStorage.getAll();
214: 		return all.filter((s) => !s.isReadOnly);
215: 	}
216: 
217: 	private async getSessionsToPoll(): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
218: 		const active = await this.sessionStorage.getActive();
219: 		const all = await this.sessionStorage.getAll();
220: 		const now = Date.now();
221: 		const activeIds = new Set(active.map((s) => s.localId));
222: 		const gracePeriodSessions: AgentSession[] = [];
223: 
224: 		for (const session of all) {
225: 			if (activeIds.has(session.localId)) {
226: 				continue;
227: 			}
228: 			if (session.isReadOnly) {
229: 				continue;
230: 			}
231: 			const completedAt = session.completedAt ?? session.updatedAt;
232: 			const hasUnknownPrState = session.pullRequests.some((pr) => !pr.state);
233: 			const effectiveGracePeriod = hasUnknownPrState
234: 				? PR_UNKNOWN_STATE_GRACE_PERIOD_MS
235: 				: PR_GRACE_PERIOD_MS;
236: 			if (now - completedAt >= effectiveGracePeriod) {
237: 				continue;
238: 			}
239: 			if (session.pullRequests.length === 0) {
240: 				continue;
241: 			}
242: 			if (
243: 				session.pullRequests.every((pr) =>
244: 					TERMINAL_PR_STATES.has(pr.state ?? "")
245: 				)
246: 			) {
247: 				continue;
248: 			}
249: 			gracePeriodSessions.push(session);
250: 		}
251: 
252: 		return [...active, ...gracePeriodSessions];
253: 	}
254: 
255: 	private async applyUpdate(update: SessionUpdate): Promise<void> {
256: 		const { localId, timestamp: _timestamp, ...fields } = update;
257: 		const isTerminal =
258: 			update.status === SessionStatus.COMPLETED ||
259: 			update.status === SessionStatus.FAILED ||
260: 			update.status === SessionStatus.CANCELLED;
261: 
262: 		let derivedTasks: AgentTask[] | undefined;
263: 		if (isTerminal && !update.tasks) {
264: 			const current = await this.sessionStorage.getById(localId);
265: 			if (current && current.tasks.length > 0) {
266: 				derivedTasks = deriveTerminalTaskStatuses(current.tasks, update.status);
267: 			}
268: 		}
269: 
270: 		await this.sessionStorage.update(localId, {
271: 			...fields,
272: 			...(derivedTasks ? { tasks: derivedTasks } : {}),
273: 			...(isTerminal ? { completedAt: Date.now() } : {}),
274: 		});
275: 
276: 		// Fan out to spec 018 subscribers AFTER the commit so readers see the
277: 		// new state. Non-terminal updates also fire so the chat panel can
278: 		// reflect incremental status/blocked transitions.
279: 		if (this.sessionUpdatedListeners.size > 0) {
280: 			const snapshot = await this.sessionStorage.getById(localId);
281: 			if (snapshot) {
282: 				this.fireSessionUpdated({ localId, session: snapshot });
283: 			}
284: 		}
285: 
286: 		if (isTerminal && update.status === SessionStatus.COMPLETED) {
287: 			const session = await this.sessionStorage.getById(localId);
288: 			if (session) {
289: 				this.callbacks.onSessionCompleted?.(localId, session.specPath);
290: 			}
291: 		}
292: 	}
293: 
294: 	private fireSessionUpdated(event: AgentSessionUpdatedEvent): void {
295: 		// Snapshot so listeners that dispose during delivery don't mutate the
296: 		// iteration.
297: 		for (const listener of [...this.sessionUpdatedListeners]) {
298: 			try {
299: 				listener(event);
300: 			} catch (error) {
301: 				logError("onSessionUpdated listener threw", error);
302: 			}
303: 		}
304: 	}
305: 
306: 	private handlePollError(error: unknown): void {
307: 		this.consecutiveFailures += 1;
308: 
309: 		if (this.isCredentialError(error)) {
310: 			logWarn("Credential expiry detected during polling");
311: 			this.callbacks.onCredentialExpiry?.();
312: 			return;
313: 		}
314: 
315: 		logError("Polling cycle failed", error);
316: 
317: 		const errorMsg =
318: 			error instanceof ProviderError
319: 				? error.message
320: 				: "Cloud agent polling failed. Check the output channel for details.";
321: 		this.callbacks.onError?.(errorMsg);
322: 
323: 		if (this.consecutiveFailures >= MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES) {
324: 			logWarn(
325: 				`Polling paused after ${MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES} consecutive failures`
326: 			);
327: 			this.stop();
328: 		}
329: 	}
330: 
331: 	private isCredentialError(error: unknown): boolean {
332: 		if (error instanceof ProviderError) {
333: 			return (
334: 				error.code === ErrorCode.CREDENTIALS_INVALID ||
335: 				error.code === ErrorCode.CREDENTIALS_MISSING
336: 			);
337: 		}
338: 		return false;
339: 	}
340: }
341: 
342: // ============================================================================
343: // Helpers
344: // ============================================================================
345: 
346: function deriveTerminalTaskStatuses(
347: 	tasks: readonly AgentTask[],
348: 	sessionStatus: SessionStatus | undefined
349: ): AgentTask[] {
350: 	let targetStatus: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.SKIPPED;
351: 	if (sessionStatus === SessionStatus.COMPLETED) {
352: 		targetStatus = TaskStatus.COMPLETED;
353: 	} else if (sessionStatus === SessionStatus.FAILED) {
354: 		targetStatus = TaskStatus.FAILED;
355: 	}
356: 
357: 	const now = Date.now();
358: 	return tasks.map((task) => {
359: 		if (
360: 			task.status === TaskStatus.COMPLETED ||
361: 			task.status === TaskStatus.FAILED ||
362: 			task.status === TaskStatus.SKIPPED
363: 		) {
364: 			return task;
365: 		}
366: 		return { ...task, status: targetStatus, completedAt: now };
367: 	});
368: }

(End of file - total 368 lines)
</content>

12: import type { Memento } from "./provider-config-store";
13: import {
14: 	SessionStatus,
15: 	TaskStatus,
16: 	type AgentSession,
17: 	type AgentTask,
18: 	type PullRequest,
19: 	StoreError,
20: 	StoreErrorCode,

22: 
23: // ============================================================================
24: // Storage Key

26: 
27: const SESSIONS_KEY = "gatomia.cloudAgent.sessions";
28: 
29: // ============================================================================
30: // AgentSessionStorage
31: // ============================================================================
32: 
33: /**
34:  * Provider-agnostic session storage using VS Code workspace state.
35:  *
36:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/storage-interface.md
37:  */
38: export class AgentSessionStorage {
39: 	private readonly workspaceState: Memento;
40: 
41: 	constructor(workspaceState: Memento) {
42: 		this.workspaceState = workspaceState;
43: 	}
44: 
45: 	/**
46: 	 * Get all sessions for the current workspace.
47: 	 */
48: 	getAll(): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
49: 		const raw = this.workspaceState.get<unknown[]>(SESSIONS_KEY);
50: 		if (!Array.isArray(raw)) {
51: 			return Promise.resolve([]);
52: 		}
53: 		const valid: AgentSession[] = [];
54: 		for (const entry of raw) {
55: 			if (isValidAgentSession(entry)) {
56: 				valid.push(normalizeSession(entry as AgentSession));
57: 			} else {
58: 				logDebug(
59: 					`Skipping invalid session entry: ${JSON.stringify(entry).slice(0, 200)}`
60: 				);
61: 			}
62: 		}
63: 		return Promise.resolve(valid);
64: 	}
65: 
66: 	/**
67: 	 * Get a single session by local ID.
68: 	 */
69: 	async getById(localId: string): Promise<AgentSession | undefined> {
70: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
71: 		return sessions.find((s) => s.localId === localId);
72: 	}
73: 
74: 	/**
75: 	 * Get sessions filtered by provider ID.
76: 	 */
77: 	async getByProvider(providerId: string): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
78: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
79: 		return sessions.filter((s) => s.providerId === providerId);
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	/**
83: 	 * Get active (non-terminal, non-read-only) sessions.
84: 	 */
85: 	async getActive(): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
86: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
87: 		return sessions.filter((s) => {
88: 			if (s.isReadOnly) {
89: 				return false;
90: 			}
91: 			switch (s.status) {
92: 				case SessionStatus.COMPLETED:
93: 				case SessionStatus.FAILED:
94: 				case SessionStatus.CANCELLED:
95: 					return false;
96: 				default:
97: 					return true;
98: 			}
99: 		});
100: 	}
101: 
102: 	/**
103: 	 * Check if a task (by specTaskId) is already in a non-terminal session.
104: 	 * Returns the running session if found, undefined otherwise.
105: 	 */
106: 	async findActiveBySpecTaskId(
107: 		specTaskId: string
108: 	): Promise<AgentSession | undefined> {
109: 		const active = await this.getActive();
110: 		return active.find((s) => s.tasks.some((t) => t.specTaskId === specTaskId));
111: 	}
112: 
113: 	/**
114: 	 * Save a new session.
115: 	 * @throws StoreError if localId already exists
116: 	 */
117: 	async create(session: AgentSession): Promise<void> {
118: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
119: 		if (sessions.some((s) => s.localId === session.localId)) {
120: 			throw new StoreError(
121: 				`Session "${session.localId}" already exists`,
122: 				StoreErrorCode.ALREADY_EXISTS,
123: 				"create"
124: 			);
125: 		}
126: 		sessions.push(session);
127: 		await this.persist(sessions);
128: 		logInfo(`Session created: ${session.localId} (${session.providerId})`);
129: 	}
130: 
131: 	/**
132: 	 * Update an existing session.

134: 	 */
135: 	async update(localId: string, updates: Partial<AgentSession>): Promise<void> {
136: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
137: 		const index = sessions.findIndex((s) => s.localId === localId);
138: 		if (index === -1) {
139: 			throw new StoreError(
140: 				`Session "${localId}" not found`,
141: 				StoreErrorCode.NOT_FOUND,
142: 				"update"
143: 			);
144: 		}
145: 		const cleaned = stripUndefined(updates);

147: 		await this.persist(sessions);
148: 		logDebug(`Session updated: ${localId}`);
149: 	}
150: 
151: 	/**
152: 	 * Delete a session.
153: 	 */
154: 	async delete(localId: string): Promise<void> {
155: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
156: 		const filtered = sessions.filter((s) => s.localId !== localId);
157: 		await this.persist(filtered);
158: 		logInfo(`Session deleted: ${localId}`);
159: 	}
160: 
161: 	/**
162: 	 * Mark all sessions from a provider as read-only.
163: 	 */
164: 	async markProviderReadOnly(providerId: string): Promise<void> {
165: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
166: 		for (const session of sessions) {
167: 			if (session.providerId === providerId) {
168: 				session.isReadOnly = true;
169: 			}
170: 		}
171: 		await this.persist(sessions);
172: 		logInfo(`All sessions for provider "${providerId}" marked read-only`);
173: 	}
174: 
175: 	/**
176: 	 * Get terminal sessions that need cleanup (updated before cutoff time).
177: 	 * Only returns sessions in completed/failed/cancelled state.
178: 	 */
179: 	async getForCleanup(cutoffTime: number): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
180: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
181: 		return sessions.filter((s) => {
182: 			const isTerminal =
183: 				s.status === SessionStatus.COMPLETED ||
184: 				s.status === SessionStatus.FAILED ||
185: 				s.status === SessionStatus.CANCELLED;
186: 			if (!isTerminal) {
187: 				return false;
188: 			}
189: 			const completionTime = s.completedAt ?? s.updatedAt;
190: 			return completionTime < cutoffTime;
191: 		});
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	private async persist(sessions: AgentSession[]): Promise<void> {
195: 		await this.workspaceState.update(SESSIONS_KEY, sessions);
196: 	}
197: }
198: 
199: // ============================================================================
200: // Validation
201: // ============================================================================
202: 
203: /**
204:  * Runtime check that a deserialized value has the minimum required shape of an AgentSession.
205:  * Filters out corrupted or schema-incompatible entries after deserialization.
206:  */
207: /**
208:  * Remove undefined values from an object to prevent overwriting
209:  * existing fields (especially arrays like tasks/pullRequests) with undefined
210:  * during a partial update spread.
211:  */
212: function stripUndefined<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(obj: T): Partial<T> {
213: 	const result: Record<string, unknown> = {};
214: 	for (const key of Object.keys(obj)) {
215: 		if (obj[key] !== undefined) {
216: 			result[key] = obj[key];
217: 		}
218: 	}
219: 	return result as Partial<T>;
220: }
221: 
222: const TERMINAL_SESSION_STATUSES: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set([
223: 	SessionStatus.COMPLETED,
224: 	SessionStatus.FAILED,
225: 	SessionStatus.CANCELLED,
226: ]);
227: 
228: const TERMINAL_TASK_STATUSES: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set([
229: 	TaskStatus.COMPLETED,
230: 	TaskStatus.FAILED,
231: 	TaskStatus.SKIPPED,
232: ]);
233: 
234: const SESSION_TO_TASK_STATUS: Record<string, TaskStatus> = {
235: 	[SessionStatus.COMPLETED]: TaskStatus.COMPLETED,
236: 	[SessionStatus.FAILED]: TaskStatus.FAILED,
237: 	[SessionStatus.CANCELLED]: TaskStatus.SKIPPED,
238: };
239: 
240: function normalizeSession(session: AgentSession): AgentSession {
241: 	if (!TERMINAL_SESSION_STATUSES.has(session.status)) {
242: 		return session;
243: 	}
244: 
245: 	let tasks: AgentTask[] | undefined;
246: 	const hasStaleTask = session.tasks.some(
247: 		(t) => !TERMINAL_TASK_STATUSES.has(t.status)
248: 	);
249: 	if (hasStaleTask) {
250: 		const targetStatus =
251: 			SESSION_TO_TASK_STATUS[session.status] ?? TaskStatus.SKIPPED;
252: 		const now = Date.now();
253: 		tasks = session.tasks.map((task) => {
254: 			if (TERMINAL_TASK_STATUSES.has(task.status)) {
255: 				return task;
256: 			}
257: 			return { ...task, status: targetStatus, completedAt: now };
258: 		});
259: 	}
260: 
261: 	let pullRequests: PullRequest[] | undefined;
262: 	const hasUnknownPrState = session.pullRequests.some((pr) => !pr.state);
263: 	if (hasUnknownPrState) {
264: 		const defaultState =
265: 			session.status === SessionStatus.COMPLETED ? "merged" : "open";
266: 		pullRequests = session.pullRequests.map((pr) =>
267: 			pr.state ? pr : { ...pr, state: defaultState }
268: 		);
269: 	}
270: 
271: 	if (tasks || pullRequests) {
272: 		return {
273: 			...session,
274: 			...(tasks ? { tasks } : {}),
275: 			...(pullRequests ? { pullRequests } : {}),
276: 		};
277: 	}
278: 	return session;
279: }
280: 
281: function isValidAgentSession(value: unknown): boolean {
282: 	if (typeof value !== "object" || value === null) {
283: 		return false;
284: 	}
285: 	const obj = value as Record<string, unknown>;
286: 	return (
287: 		typeof obj.localId === "string" &&
288: 		typeof obj.providerId === "string" &&
289: 		typeof obj.status === "string" &&
290: 		typeof obj.branch === "string" &&
291: 		typeof obj.specPath === "string" &&
292: 		typeof obj.createdAt === "number" &&
293: 		typeof obj.updatedAt === "number" &&
294: 		Array.isArray(obj.tasks) &&
295: 		Array.isArray(obj.pullRequests)
296: 	);
297: }

275: interface ExtractedTask {
276: 	taskId: string;
277: 	taskTitle: string;

283: function extractTaskFromItem(
284: 	itemOrTaskId: DispatchTreeItem | string | undefined,
285: 	specPathArg?: string
286: ): ExtractedTask | undefined {
287: 	if (typeof itemOrTaskId === "object" && itemOrTaskId !== null) {
288: 		const item = itemOrTaskId;
289: 		if (item.task) {
290: 			return {
291: 				taskId: item.task.id,
292: 				taskTitle: item.task.title,
293: 				taskDescription: `${item.task.id}: ${item.task.title}`,
294: 				path: item.filePath ?? "",
295: 				isTaskGroup: false,
296: 			};
297: 		}
298: 		if (item.contextValue === "task-group" && item.parentName) {
299: 			return {
300: 				taskId: item.parentName,
301: 				taskTitle: item.parentName,
302: 				taskDescription: `Task group: ${item.parentName}`,
303: 				path: item.filePath ?? "",
304: 				isTaskGroup: true,
305: 			};
306: 		}
307: 		return;
308: 	}
309: 	const taskId = itemOrTaskId ?? "task";
310: 	return {
311: 		taskId,
312: 		taskTitle: taskId,
313: 		taskDescription: `Dispatch task ${taskId}`,
314: 		path: specPathArg ?? "",
315: 		isTaskGroup: false,
316: 	};
317: }
318: 
319: async function ensureActiveProvider(
320: 	registry: ProviderRegistry
321: ): Promise<CloudAgentProvider | undefined> {
322: 	let provider = registry.getActive();
323: 	if (!provider) {
324: 		window.showInformationMessage(
325: 			"No provider selected. Please select a provider first."
326: 		);
327: 		await commands.executeCommand(CLOUD_AGENT_COMMANDS.SELECT_PROVIDER);
328: 		provider = registry.getActive();
329: 		if (!provider) {
330: 			return;
331: 		}
332: 	}
333: 	const hasCreds = await provider.hasCredentials();
334: 	if (!hasCreds) {
335: 		window.showInformationMessage(
336: 			`Please configure ${provider.metadata.displayName} credentials first.`
337: 		);
338: 		await commands.executeCommand(
339: 			CLOUD_AGENT_COMMANDS.CONFIGURE_PROVIDER,
340: 			provider.metadata.id
341: 		);
342: 		return;
343: 	}

347: async function handleDispatchTask(
348: 	opts: CloudAgentCommandOptions,
349: 	itemOrTaskId?: DispatchTreeItem | string,
350: 	specPathArg?: string
351: ): Promise<void> {
352: 	try {
353: 		const provider = await ensureActiveProvider(opts.registry);
354: 		if (!provider) {
355: 			return;
356: 		}
357: 
358: 		const extracted = extractTaskFromItem(itemOrTaskId, specPathArg);
359: 		if (!extracted) {
360: 			window.showErrorMessage(
361: 				"No task selected. Use this action from the Spec Explorer task list."
362: 			);
363: 			return;
364: 		}
365: 
366: 		if (await isTaskAlreadyRunning(opts.sessionStorage, extracted)) {
367: 			return;
368: 		}
369: 
370: 		const task: SpecTask = {
371: 			id: extracted.taskId,
372: 			title: extracted.taskTitle,
373: 			description: extracted.taskDescription,
374: 			priority: "medium",
375: 		};
376: 		const branch = (await getCurrentBranch()) ?? "main";
377: 		const workspaceUri = workspace.workspaceFolders?.[0]?.uri.toString() ?? "";
378: 		const repoUrl = (await getRemoteUrl()) ?? "";
379: 		const featurePath = computeFeaturePath(extracted.path);
380: 		const context: SessionContext = {
381: 			branch,
382: 			specPath: extracted.path,
383: 			workspaceUri,
384: 			repoUrl,
385: 			featurePath,
386: 			isFullFeature: false,
387: 			taskIds: [extracted.taskId],
388: 		};
389: 
390: 		const session = await createSessionWithRetry(provider, task, context);
391: 
392: 		if (opts.sessionStorage) {
393: 			await opts.sessionStorage.create(session);
394: 		}
395: 		if (opts.pollingService && !opts.pollingService.isRunning) {
396: 			opts.pollingService.start(30_000);
397: 		}
398: 
399: 		opts.onSessionCreated?.();
400: 		opts.onRefresh?.();
401: 
402: 		logInfo(`Task dispatched to ${provider.metadata.id}: ${extracted.taskId}`);
403: 		window.showInformationMessage(
404: 			`Task "${extracted.taskTitle}" dispatched to ${provider.metadata.displayName}.`
405: 		);
406: 	} catch (error) {
407: 		logError("Failed to dispatch task", error);
408: 		const reason = extractErrorMessage(error);
409: 		window.showErrorMessage(`Failed to dispatch task: ${reason}`);
410: 	}
411: }
412: 
413: const MAX_DISPATCH_RETRIES = 2;
414: const RETRY_DELAY_MS = 1000;
415: 
416: async function createSessionWithRetry(
417: 	provider: CloudAgentProvider,
418: 	task: SpecTask,
419: 	context: SessionContext
420: ): Promise<AgentSession> {
421: 	let lastError: unknown;
422: 	for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= MAX_DISPATCH_RETRIES; attempt++) {
423: 		try {
424: 			return await provider.createSession(task, context);
425: 		} catch (error) {
426: 			lastError = error;
427: 			const isRecoverable = error instanceof ProviderError && error.recoverable;
428: 			if (!isRecoverable || attempt === MAX_DISPATCH_RETRIES) {
429: 				throw error;
430: 			}
431: 			logError(`Dispatch attempt ${attempt + 1} failed, retrying...`, error);
432: 			await new Promise((resolve) => {
433: 				setTimeout(resolve, RETRY_DELAY_MS * (attempt + 1));
434: 			});
435: 		}
436: 	}
437: 	throw lastError;
438: }

440: async function isTaskAlreadyRunning(
441: 	sessionStorage: AgentSessionStorage | undefined,
442: 	extracted: { taskId: string; taskTitle: string }
443: ): Promise<boolean> {
444: 	if (!sessionStorage) {
445: 		return false;
446: 	}
447: 	const running = await sessionStorage.findActiveBySpecTaskId(extracted.taskId);
448: 	if (!running) {
449: 		return false;
450: 	}
451: 	const action = await window.showWarningMessage(
452: 		`Task "${extracted.taskTitle}" is already running on ${running.providerId} (status: ${running.status}).`,
453: 		"Open Session",
454: 		"Cancel"
455: 	);
456: 	if (action === "Open Session" && running.externalUrl) {
457: 		await commands.executeCommand(
458: 			"vscode.open",
459: 			Uri.parse(running.externalUrl)
460: 		);
461: 	}
462: 	return true;
463: }

466: 	opts: CloudAgentCommandOptions,

473: 		}
474: 
475: 		const path = item?.filePath ?? "";

483:

485: 			id: specName,
486: 			title: `Full Spec: ${specName}`,
487: 			description: `Implement the entire feature: ${specName}`,
488: 			priority: "medium",
489: 		};
490: 		const branch = (await getCurrentBranch()) ?? "main";
491: 		const workspaceUri = workspace.workspaceFolders?.[0]?.uri.toString() ?? "";
492: 		const repoUrl = (await getRemoteUrl()) ?? "";
493: 		const context: SessionContext = {
494: 			branch,
495: 			specPath: path,
496: 			workspaceUri,
497: 			repoUrl,
498: 			featurePath,
499: 			isFullFeature: true,
500: 		};
501: 
502: 		const session = await createSessionWithRetry(provider, task, context);
503: 
504: 		if (opts.sessionStorage) {
505: 			await opts.sessionStorage.create(session);
506: 		}
507: 		if (opts.pollingService && !opts.pollingService.isRunning) {
508: 			opts.pollingService.start(30_000);
509: 		}
510: 
511: 		opts.onSessionCreated?.();
512: 		opts.onRefresh?.();
513: 
514: 		logInfo(`Full spec dispatched to ${provider.metadata.id}: ${specName}`);
515: 		window.showInformationMessage(
516: 			`Full spec "${specName}" dispatched to ${provider.metadata.displayName}.`
517: 		);
518: 	} catch (error) {
519: 		logError("Failed to dispatch full spec", error);
520: 		const reason = extractErrorMessage(error);
521: 		window.showErrorMessage(`Failed to dispatch full spec: ${reason}`);
522: 	}
523: }

525: async function handleRefresh(opts: CloudAgentCommandOptions): Promise<void> {
526: 	try {
527: 		if (opts.pollingService) {
528: 			await opts.pollingService.pollOnce(true);
529: 		}
530: 		opts.onRefresh?.();
531: 		logInfo("Cloud Agent sessions refreshed");
532: 	} catch (error) {
533: 		logError("Failed to refresh cloud agent sessions", error);
534: 	}

537: async function handleCancelSession(
538: 	opts: CloudAgentCommandOptions,
539: 	localId?: string
540: ): Promise<void> {
541: 	const { registry, sessionStorage, onRefresh } = opts;
542: 	try {
543: 		if (!localId) {
544: 			return;
545: 		}
546: 		if (!sessionStorage) {
547: 			return;
548: 		}
549: 
550: 		const session = await sessionStorage.getById(localId);
551: 		if (!session) {
552: 			return;
553: 		}
554: 
555: 		if (session.isReadOnly) {
556: 			window.showWarningMessage(
557: 				"Cannot cancel a read-only session from a previous provider."
558: 			);
559: 			return;
560: 		}
561: 
562: 		const provider = registry.get(session.providerId);
563: 		if (provider && session.localId) {
564: 			await provider.cancelSession(session.localId);
565: 		}
566: 
567: 		await sessionStorage.update(localId, {
568: 			status: SessionStatus.CANCELLED,
569: 			completedAt: Date.now(),
570: 		});
571: 		onRefresh?.();
572: 		logInfo(`Session cancelled: ${localId}`);
573: 		window.showInformationMessage("Session cancelled.");
574: 	} catch (error) {
575: 		logError("Failed to cancel session", error);
576: 		window.showErrorMessage("Failed to cancel session.");
577: 	}
578: }

**Evaluating product states**

I'm working on identifying product surface states, and I'm considering whether the snapshot should include provider availability and failure statuses. The task mentions needing actionable states such as empty, loading, or degraded, along with explanations for no sessions or missing providers. I could enhance the snapshot with extra degraded reasons and check for active provider presence. It might be easiest to manage UI updates based on `activeProvider` and session counts, like displaying "No cloud provider connected" when neither is available.

**Exploring tree updates**

I'm examining how tree updates could respond to changes in session storage, registry, and cloud provider changes without needing a manual reload. I’m considering adding `onDidChange` to both `ProviderRegistry` and `AgentSessionStorage` to refresh the tree and orchestration based on store changes, not just polling updates. I might also introduce an event emitter for `AgentSessionStorage` that triggers on persist. This approach might make the system more aligned and efficient, even if it requires additional work. I'll need to inspect `AgentChatSessionStore` too.

**Streamlining event handling**

I could simplify things by adding an `onDidChange` event to `AgentSessionStorage` to trigger on various actions like create, update, delete, and persistence. Similarly, I could implement this for `ProviderRegistry` to respond to actions such as register, setActive, and clearActive if there are any changes. In the `CloudAgentProgressProvider`, I would subscribe to those events to refresh and update context keys, which would streamline tree updates. Lastly, I might reconsider whether I need the polling service if the session storage changes are sufficient.

**Planning Unit Tests**

I’m considering whether I need unit tests for events in ProviderRegistry and AgentSessionStorage. I should think about testing scenarios when getAll from the cloud throws an error, as well as handling change events triggering onDidChange. There’s also the idea of testing a webview for scenarios with no provider and degradation cases. It seems the task mentions checking a current checkbox, but I’ll double-check if provider registry tests already exist and see if I can find agent-session-storage tests using glob.

I’m extending the existing state sources instead of adding another orchestration layer: cloud storage and provider registry will emit real change events, then the tree and orchestration view can both react to the same source-of-truth updates.

# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when a beads workspace is resolved

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)
- `bd doctor --check=conventions` - Check for convention drift (lint, stale, orphans)

### Quality Tools
- `bd create --validate` - Check description has required sections
- `bd create --acceptance="criteria"` - Set acceptance criteria (checked by --validate)
- `bd create --design="decisions"` - Record design decisions
- `bd create --notes="context"` - Add supplementary notes
- `bd config set validation.on-create warn` - Auto-validate on every create
- `bd lint` - Check existing issues for missing sections

### Lifecycle & Hygiene
- `bd defer <id> --until="date"` - Defer work to a future date
- `bd supersede <id> --with=<new-id>` - Mark issue as superseded
- `bd close <id> --suggest-next` - Show newly unblocked issues after closing
- `bd stale` - Find issues with no recent activity
- `bd orphans` - Find issues with broken dependencies
- `bd preflight` - Pre-PR checks (lint, stale, orphans)
- `bd human <id>` - Flag for human decision (list/respond/dismiss)

### Structured Workflows
- `bd formula list` - See available workflow templates
- `bd mol pour <name>` - Start structured workflow from formula

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

1: /**
2:  * Provider Registry

4:  * Manages registration and active-provider orchestration for cloud agent providers.

13: import { logInfo, logError } from "./logging";
14: import type { ProviderConfigStore } from "./provider-config-store";
15: 
16: // ============================================================================
17: // ProviderRegistry
18: // ============================================================================
19: 
20: /**
21:  * Registry for cloud agent provider adapters.
22:  * Manages available providers and active provider state.
23:  *
24:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/provider-interface.md
25:  */
26: export class ProviderRegistry {
27: 	private readonly providers = new Map<string, CloudAgentProvider>();
28: 	private readonly configStore: ProviderConfigStore;
29: 	private activeProviderId: string | undefined;
30: 	private readonly _onDidChange = new EventEmitter<void>();
31: 	readonly onDidChange: Event<void> = this._onDidChange.event;
32: 
33: 	constructor(configStore: ProviderConfigStore) {
34: 		this.configStore = configStore;
35: 	}
36: 
37: 	/**
38: 	 * Register a provider adapter. Throws if a provider with the same ID is already registered.
39: 	 * @param provider - The provider to register
40: 	 */
41: 	register(provider: CloudAgentProvider): void {
42: 		const id = provider.metadata.id;
43: 		if (this.providers.has(id)) {
44: 			throw new Error(`Provider "${id}" is already registered`);
45: 		}
46: 		this.providers.set(id, provider);

49: 	}

53: 	 * @param id - The provider ID
54: 	 * @returns The provider or undefined if not found
55: 	 */

57: 		return this.providers.get(id);
58: 	}

12: import { logInfo, logDebug } from "./logging";
13: import type { Memento } from "./provider-config-store";
14: import {

16: 	TaskStatus,

20: 	StoreError,
21: 	StoreErrorCode,

23: 
24: // ============================================================================
25: // Storage Key
26: // ============================================================================
27: 
28: const SESSIONS_KEY = "gatomia.cloudAgent.sessions";
29: 
30: // ============================================================================
31: // AgentSessionStorage
32: // ============================================================================
33: 
34: /**
35:  * Provider-agnostic session storage using VS Code workspace state.
36:  *
37:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/storage-interface.md
38:  */
39: export class AgentSessionStorage {
40: 	private readonly workspaceState: Memento;
41: 	private readonly _onDidChange = new EventEmitter<void>();
42: 	readonly onDidChange: Event<void> = this._onDidChange.event;
43: 
44: 	constructor(workspaceState: Memento) {
45: 		this.workspaceState = workspaceState;
46: 	}
47: 
48: 	/**
49: 	 * Get all sessions for the current workspace.
50: 	 */
51: 	getAll(): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
52: 		const raw = this.workspaceState.get<unknown[]>(SESSIONS_KEY);
53: 		if (!Array.isArray(raw)) {
54: 			return Promise.resolve([]);
55: 		}
56: 		const valid: AgentSession[] = [];
57: 		for (const entry of raw) {
58: 			if (isValidAgentSession(entry)) {
59: 				valid.push(normalizeSession(entry as AgentSession));
60: 			} else {
61: 				logDebug(
62: 					`Skipping invalid session entry: ${JSON.stringify(entry).slice(0, 200)}`
63: 				);
64: 			}
65: 		}
66: 		return Promise.resolve(valid);
67: 	}
68: 
69: 	/**
70: 	 * Get a single session by local ID.
71: 	 */
72: 	async getById(localId: string): Promise<AgentSession | undefined> {
73: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
74: 		return sessions.find((s) => s.localId === localId);
75: 	}
76: 
77: 	/**
78: 	 * Get sessions filtered by provider ID.
79: 	 */
80: 	async getByProvider(providerId: string): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
81: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
82: 		return sessions.filter((s) => s.providerId === providerId);
83: 	}
84: 
85: 	/**
86: 	 * Get active (non-terminal, non-read-only) sessions.
87: 	 */
88: 	async getActive(): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
89: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
90: 		return sessions.filter((s) => {
91: 			if (s.isReadOnly) {
92: 				return false;
93: 			}
94: 			switch (s.status) {
95: 				case SessionStatus.COMPLETED:
96: 				case SessionStatus.FAILED:
97: 				case SessionStatus.CANCELLED:
98: 					return false;
99: 				default:
100: 					return true;
101: 			}
102: 		});
103: 	}
104: 
105: 	/**
106: 	 * Check if a task (by specTaskId) is already in a non-terminal session.
107: 	 * Returns the running session if found, undefined otherwise.
108: 	 */
109: 	async findActiveBySpecTaskId(
110: 		specTaskId: string
111: 	): Promise<AgentSession | undefined> {
112: 		const active = await this.getActive();
113: 		return active.find((s) => s.tasks.some((t) => t.specTaskId === specTaskId));
114: 	}
115: 
116: 	/**
117: 	 * Save a new session.
118: 	 * @throws StoreError if localId already exists
119: 	 */
120: 	async create(session: AgentSession): Promise<void> {
121: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
122: 		if (sessions.some((s) => s.localId === session.localId)) {
123: 			throw new StoreError(
124: 				`Session "${session.localId}" already exists`,
125: 				StoreErrorCode.ALREADY_EXISTS,

132: 	}
133: 
134: 	/**
